name: Kunun Gyada
intro: A northern Nigerian porridge made with groundnuts and rice. This filling porridge is a great source of protein and carbohydrates, and can be eaten at any time of the day, but especially as a warming breakfast alongside some Akara/Kosai (page 135) or Masa (page 175).

ingredients.item: 1/2 cup white short-grain rice (soaked overnight then drained)

method: Place the groundnuts in a food blender with 2 cups of water and blend until you have a smooth paste. Sieve the mixture into a pot to extract the milk. You can repurpose the leftover pulp in other recipes such as Kuli Kuli (page 152) or add to cakes, muﬃns or pancakes.

Add the rice and tamarind paste to the groundnut milk, along with 1 cup of water. Simmer uncovered for 20-25 minutes on medium-low heat, stirring frequently so the rice does not stick to the bottom of the pot.
Serve warm on its own or with a side of Akara/Kosai (page 135), Masa (page 175) or Moin Moin (page 156).
